summary: Install-time exfiltration of SSH directory contents and host identifiers to hardcoded IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js enumerates the installer's ~/.ssh directory (collecting public key filenames) along with the OS username and platform, and POSTs the data over HTTPS to the hardcoded bare IP 124.221.154.135:443 at path /post. The exfiltration fires unconditionally from the postinstall lifecycle hook, so any developer or CI system running `npm install houzidawang806` triggers the leak without any further interaction. The package's declared purpose is a date-formatting utility, which has no relation to reading ~/.ssh or contacting an arbitrary remote host. Source comments self-describe the destination as C2 infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
